summary:
The recovery logic in `wal.go` stops reading entries on any error (including a single corrupt or partial entry after a crash), possibly causing all subsequent valid entries to be ignored—this could be the “needle” problem.

A better approach: only stop recovery for an unrecoverable error (not a truncated/incomplete final entry), and otherwise recover all complete valid entries up to the corruption. I’ll patch `Recover` to check error types, allowing it to ignore EOF or certain decoding errors, and return all successfully recovered entries.

Next, I’ll update `wal.go` so that `Recover` only skips the bad/corrupt entry at the end (commonly after a crash) and still returns previous valid entries.
